# host_fstab_entry (Resource)

Manages one provider-owned entry block in `/etc/fstab`.

This resource updates only the named Terraform-managed block. It does not mount or unmount filesystems.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_fstab_entry" "data" {
  name        = "data"
  device      = "UUID=11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555"
  mount_point = "/data"
  fs_type     = "ext4"
  options     = "defaults,noatime"
  pass        = 2
}
```

## Import

Import an existing Terraform-managed fstab block by name:

```shell
terraform import host_fstab_entry.data data
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `device` (String) Fstab device field, such as `UUID=...` or `/dev/disk/by-label/data`.
- `fs_type` (String) Filesystem type field, such as `ext4`, `btrfs`, `xfs`, or `swap`.
- `mount_point` (String) Mount point field. Use an absolute path or `none`.
- `name` (String) Stable name for this managed fstab entry block.

### Optional

- `dump` (Number) Fstab dump field.
- `options` (String) Fstab options field.
- `pass` (Number) Fstab pass field.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `path` (String) Managed fstab path.
